deductions.reason: The tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—no statement about active reasoning, retention, or skill development appears in any of the three transcripts. Constraints are enforced but the rationale for the tutoring approach is never articulated.

deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1 Turn 10, Transcript 2 Turn 10, Transcript 3 Turn 10
deductions.reason: In all three transcripts the student makes clear progress (e.g., correctly articulating source-selection rules, drafting a table skeleton, resolving the GDP labeling question), but the tutor never pauses to prompt reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the improved approach worked. It simply moves to the next task.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts the tutor performs strongly: it never gives submission-ready answers, consistently uses Socratic questioning, scaffolds well from student responses, maintains appropriate tone, and keeps responses concise and focused. Two deductions apply: the tutor never explains the rationale for its guided-discovery approach (1.3.A.a), and it consistently misses opportunities to prompt reflection after student improvement (1.3.C.a). No other rubric criteria are clearly triggered.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never provides final answers or near-submission-ready content; all key reasoning steps are left to the student, and the tutor only validates or sharpens student-generated ideas. No deductions apply. 1.2: The tutor consistently builds on correct student progress and asks diagnostic questions before redirecting; no deductions apply. 1.3: Two deductions are warranted. First, across all three conversations the tutor never articulates why it is guiding rather than telling—no mention of learning rationale, retention, or skill-building (1.3.A.a, -2). Second, whenever students reach a correct conclusion after working through confusion (e.g., the GDP labeling resolution, the table skeleton in T2, the ACS consistency reasoning in T3), the tutor immediately pivots to the next task without prompting any reflection on what changed or why the improved thinking worked (1.3.C.a, -2). 1.3.B is not triggered because the tutor gives specific, process-level feedback throughout. Dialogue quality and communication quality are both strong with no evidence of repetition without progress, assignment drift, monologue-style responses, or tone issues.
